review: Peat learning-curriculum hardening to enterprise-onboarding grade
phases_completed: Phase 1 — Ground truth established (per-repo audits + merged ground-truth.md)
Phase 2 — Adversarial audit of the curriculum (14 per-document ledgers + merged claim-ledger.md)
Phase 5 — Use cases & examples (five worked, leg-by-leg labeled)
Phase 6 — Curriculum rewrite (hub + constrained track + 12 markdown modules, in place)

gbrain_pages.note: All writes to the default source. No Peat doc page deleted (memory: keep-peat-docs-in-gbrain-default). Pages cross-linked with wikilinks; put_page reported created_or_updated + write_through.written:true for all three.

peat.head: 2778bd9
peat.subject: add: ADR review of Rayfish handling of peer admission/ejection (#1021)
peat.version: workspace 0.9.0-rc.29; peat-ffi 0.2.10; peat-schema =0.9.0-rc.29; peat-mesh floor >=0.9.0-rc.45 (git [patch.crates-io] pin DROPPED #1016 — registry crate now)
peat.origin_status: advanced 871776d->2778bd9 this run (11 commits). Shipped/additive: UniFFI blob surface + poll-based blob_fetch_start->Arc<BlobFetchHandle> #1017 (peat-ffi/src/lib.rs:3358+; Some(peer)->direct fetch_blob_from_peer, None->mesh-sync fetch_blob); Android mDNS bindAddress + formation-identity through createNodeJni JNI #1006 (BREAKING JNI arity) + dial mDNS-discovered peers via _peat._udp browse consumer #1007 (sync/automerge.rs:2682). Dropped [patch.crates-io] peat-mesh git-pin + raised floor to rc.45 #1016. ADR-073 peer-ejection Rayfish review [Proposed, NO code, no change to ADR-056] #1021 -> ADR total 78->79. examples/peat-tak-bridge MOVED to standalone peat-tak repo (=0.0.2) #1020. rc.29 release #1019. cot/event.rs quick-xml 0.37->0.41 (RUSTSEC-2026-0194/0195, behaviour-equivalent). CellRole still 7 (models/role.rs:14-30); 11 Accepted ADRs UNCHANGED (002,009,015,016,023,024,030,041,047,057,070 — grep BOTH **Status:**/**Status**: formats). FIPS posture unchanged (no crypto added). Maven AAR version for rc.29 not derivable from tree (logged, not asserted).

peat-mesh.head: b410d7c
peat-mesh.subject: chore(release): 0.9.0-rc.45 (#275)
peat-mesh.version: 0.9.0-rc.45 (#273 rc.44, #275 rc.45; README carries only illustrative 0.1.0/0.2.0 examples)
peat-mesh.origin_status: advanced c863d16->b410d7c this run (14 commits). Shipped: fetch_blob_from_peer — direct, pull-only, single caller-chosen peer, bypasses candidate list + PeerHealthIndex, NO fallback (storage/iroh_blob_store.rs:1497, peat-mesh#274); add_peer_from_hex_id — register a peer by id only, no static addr (:1347, #270); Android mDNS discovered-peer dial #268 (advertise dialable hex EndpointId iroh_transport.rs:836; From<discovery::PeerInfo> for dialable PeerInfo peer_info.rs:108; is_routable_addr drops loopback/link-local :73; self-respawning supervised browse daemon discovery/mdns.rs). Provider gossip (blob-announce/1, DEFAULT_ANNOUNCE_TTL=3, classify_announce) UNCHANGED — direct fetch lives alongside and re-announces on success. NO crypto change (derive_iroh_node_secret still HKDF-SHA-256 iroh_transport.rs:128; no ChaCha20/X25519). NEEDS_RUNTIME: direct-fetch transfer + browse recovery code-confirmed, not benchmarked.

peat-sapient.head: 5118afa
peat-sapient.subject: fix: DLMM reconnect after HLDMM drops connection (#40)
peat-sapient.version: 0.1.0; now a 3-crate workspace (peat-sapient / peat-mesh-sapient / peat-sapient-bridge); peat-schema =0.9.0-rc.24 (TRAILS umbrella rc.29 by 5); peat-mesh =rc.41; consumes peat-tak =0.0.2. SAPIENT BSI Flex 335 v2.0 (proto pinned dstl 244a741).
peat-sapient.origin_status: advanced 8ec24d4->5118afa this run (13 commits — MAJOR restructure). Now a 3-crate workspace: peat-sapient (core codec+transforms+C2, peat-schema OPTIONAL — corrects prior 'peat-schema ONLY'), peat-mesh-sapient (one-way Translator/Transport onto peat-mesh, ADR-059 Amend.4), peat-sapient-bridge (deployable bidirectional SAPIENT<->mesh<->TAK binary). OPT-IN TLS #39 (default-off, config.*.tls unwrap_or(false), main.rs:132,164; plain TCP still supported) for SAPIENT DLMM<->HLDMM + TAK links (mTLS via peat_tak::TakIdentity) — FLIPS prior 'plain TCP, NO TLS' flag. SAPIENT->mesh->TAK/CoT via Translator #28 (DetectionReport->tracks, Registration/StatusReport->platforms). AlertAck #31, command_id correlation #33, watchdog #35, rate limit, DLMM reconnect #40, benchmarks. FIPS FLAG: fips_crypto_provider (connection.rs:337-348) restricts cipher suites to AES-GCM but leaves aws_lc_rs::default_provider() kx_groups intact so X25519 (non-FIPS) STILL OFFERED; uses default_provider NOT the aws-lc-rs fips module — source-level (all crates 0.1.0, unpublished). NEEDS_RUNTIME: actual negotiated kx group. Ground truth: review/ground-truth/peat-sapient.md 2026-07-06 delta.

open_todos: gbrain refresh PENDING (the user's next step): apply C1 (BLE FIPS published-vs-source split), C2 (drop peat-btle#75/aws-lc-rs), C11 (peat-schema proto renamed off Squad*), C12 (11 Accepted ADRs incl ADR-070 SAPIENT), C13 (ADR-006/044 FIPS-amended) — plus the two new repos + tombstone-GC-shipped + ADR-count-79 + the 2026-07-06 deltas (peat-mesh fetch_blob_from_peer, peat-ffi blob surface, peat-sapient 3-crate + opt-in TLS flip, peat-tak repo split) — to peat-current-state, peat-hierarchy-vocabulary, and peat-open-issues-snapshot.

Curriculum integration of the two newer repos (now MORE pressing after the 2026-07-06 peat-sapient restructure): write Module '04c client bindings' (peat-flutter) and a full Module 7 SAPIENT-bridge case study + Module 9 BSI Flex 335 v2.0 wire-format section (peat-sapient is now a 3-crate workspace with opt-in TLS — ADR-070 ACCEPTED, first-class integration). This incremental only updated the Module 7 §7.1 row + §7.8 FIPS note; the standalone sections remain full-sweep work.

NEW REPO peat-tak (github.com/defenseunicorns/peat-tak): the TAK/CoT bridge example moved out of peat/examples (peat#1020) into a standalone published crate (=0.0.2), consumed by peat-sapient-bridge. NOT yet in the tracked-clone set or the prompt routing tables — consider folding it in (it now owns the runnable TAK bridge the curriculum points at from Modules 2/6). Detected on a CI-mode run; adding it to tracking is a full-sweep §1c task (mechanical routing-table add).
peat-node QUICKSTART.md (root = node/container) + crates/peat-cli/QUICKSTART.md (CLI) — route into Module 8 + a getting-started callout in Module 0/0b (Kit, 2026-06-24).

Leader-election score-over-message convergence (peat-protocol/src/cell/leader_election.rs ~340-345): code carries a 'follows higher node id' tiebreak + 'future work' comment; NO tracking issue located — file one upstream or log as a known design gap.

peat-sapient peat-schema requirement (0.9.0-rc.24, a NON-exact caret req — no leading '=', unlike the peat repo's exact =0.9.0-rc.29) trails the umbrella (rc.29) by 5 rc's, and its internal pins are now inconsistent (peat-mesh =rc.41, peat-protocol dev-dep =rc.28); re-check each sync (a bump may rename transform fields, e.g. NodeState.health).

peat-sapient TLS FIPS follow-up: fips_crypto_provider() restricts cipher suites to AES-GCM but leaves aws_lc_rs::default_provider() kx_groups intact (X25519 still offered) and does not use the aws-lc-rs fips module. Watch for a switch to the fips provider + kx-group restriction (P-256/P-384 only) — the same X25519-for-FIPS-review theme as ADR-044.

ADR-044 MLS key-exchange: X25519 still flagged for FIPS review (amendment 2026-05-18 prefers ECDH-P256/P384 'where the design has a choice'; tracked as MLS-integration follow-up) — watch for resolution.
unverifiable_claims: EXTERNAL (registry): crates.io / Maven Central publication status of any Peat crate — needs registry access. NOTE the verified version-collision: the crates.io-published peat-btle 0.4.0 (checksum a57dd351) ships non-FIPS ChaCha20/X25519 while local git 0.4.0 (3d70f48) is FIPS-clean (commit c8b013e) — same version string, FIPS migration never re-published.

EXTERNAL: standalone repos peat-sim / peat-registry / peat-sdk-go / standalone peat-android are NOT present as local dirs under ~/code/peat; existence/status needs GitHub. (peat-sim is referenced by ADR-057 'peat-sim scaling plans', Accepted — so it is at least a planned/accepted artifact.)

EXTERNAL hardware specs: LoRa 7-87 km / 1.5-9.1 kB/s and Iridium SBD ~1960 B / ~$0.04-0.13/msg are vendor/spec numbers (ADR-052 LoRa Proposed). CONFIRMED from code: there is NO LoRa or SBD transport implementation (Proposed only).
NEEDS_RUNTIME: '1,000+ node' validation is a single-machine SIM ceiling (1023), per Peat-Lab-Experiments-Whitepaper — not a field result; needs a live run to re-confirm.

NEEDS_RUNTIME: '<5s P1 latency' (README:232) and the bandwidth-reduction % figures are targets/benchmarks — require a live build/benchmark, not a code read. QoS BandwidthAllocation shares ARE in code (peat-mesh/src/qos/bandwidth.rs:250-300) but enforcement/latency is not.

NEEDS_RUNTIME: provider-gossip multi-hop reach under partition (peat/blob-announce/1, DEFAULT_ANNOUNCE_TTL=3) — code paths CONFIRMED (blob_announce.rs, BlobPeerIndex, iroh_blob_store.rs:1544-1663) but actual multi-hop delivery under partition is not benchmarked.

NEEDS_RUNTIME: peat-node attachment outbox watcher + inbox layout-mirror + post-write blob_size validation + path re-sanitisation — code paths CONFIRMED (src/attachments/*); runtime behavior not exercised live.
DECLARED-CONSTANT (not measured): peat-btle BLE rate/range (~100-400 m, 125 kbps-2 Mbps) are capability constants in code, not measured field results.

NEEDS_RUNTIME (2026-06-29): the peat-ffi 0.2.9 per-peer reconnect supervisor (backoff timing 2s->5min, deterministic jitter, <=8 concurrent dials, cross-transport dedup), the persistent peer roster, the origin-tagged DocumentChange routing, and the peat-mesh#266 Android _peat._udp peat-controlled browse are all CONFIRMED from source (peat-ffi/src/{supervisor,roster,lib}.rs; peat-mesh/src/network/iroh_transport.rs + tests/peat_mdns_browse.rs) but NOT exercised live here — actual backoff/dedup behaviour and Android browse delivery are not benchmarked.

NEEDS_RUNTIME (2026-07-06): the rc.45 peat-mesh direct blob path (fetch_blob_from_peer single-peer no-fallback pull, iroh_blob_store.rs:1497; add_peer_from_hex_id :1347) and the rc.29 peat-ffi poll-based blob surface (blob_fetch_start -> Arc<BlobFetchHandle>.status(), lib.rs:3358+) are CONFIRMED from source (+ a feature-gated e2e test blob_direct_peer_fetch_e2e.rs) but the actual transfer/backpressure/poll behaviour is not benchmarked here. Also NEEDS_RUNTIME: the rc.44-rc.45 Android mDNS discovered-peer dial + self-respawning browse-daemon recovery (peat-mesh#268; peat#1006/#1007) — dialability and panic-recovery are code-confirmed, not exercised on-device.

NEEDS_RUNTIME (2026-07-06): peat-sapient's opt-in TLS (peat-sapient#39) restricts cipher suites to AES-GCM (fips_crypto_provider, connection.rs:337-348) but spreads aws_lc_rs::default_provider() kx_groups unchanged, so X25519 (non-FIPS) is OFFERED by construction; which key-exchange group is ACTUALLY negotiated needs a runtime handshake capture. Also note: it uses the standard aws-lc-rs provider, NOT the fips module (a static fact, not runtime) — so 'FIPS-approved cipher suites' overstates the shipped posture. Source-level flag; all peat-sapient crates are 0.1.0 and unpublished.

verification_2026-06-26.method: 8-repo verification workflow (33 agents): 7 subsystem verifiers + adversarial re-verification of high-stakes/label-changing findings + 3 discovery rounds. Repos read at audited HEADs. 67 verdicts (43 confirmed, 18 refuted/corrected, 6 external/runtime); 36 discovery issues. All contradictory-to-prior-belief findings self-re-verified by grep.
verification_2026-06-26.convergence_note: CONVERGED for the reader-facing curriculum (12 modules + index.html + peat-constrained-networking.html) and the canonical ground-truth (review/ground-truth.md + review/ground-truth/*.md). Two fix workflows (16 + 12 agents) applied 44 + 34 edits; a final grep sweep for the five recurring stale tokens (CellRole-6, SquadSummary-still-shipping, peat-btle#75/aws-lc-rs, peat-mesh hierarchy/router.rs, RPC 28/27) returns clean. NOTE: peat-protocol/src/hierarchy/router.rs (is_route_valid:101) is a REAL file and its citations are correct. gbrain corrections (C1/C2/C11/C12/C13 -> peat-current-state §5/§6/§9, peat-hierarchy-vocabulary, peat-open-issues-snapshot) are deferred to the gbrain-refresh step.

corrections.what: Shipped BLE crypto is NOT FIPS-clean (published-vs-source split)
corrections.truth: peat-btle SOURCE (HEAD 3d70f48) is FIPS-clean: AES-256-GCM + ECDH P-256 (migration commit c8b013e). But the crates.io-PUBLISHED peat-btle 0.4.0 (checksum a57dd351) that peat-flutter builds still depends on chacha20poly1305 + x25519-dalek. Same version string, FIPS migration never re-published.
corrections.status: applied (curriculum + ground-truth); gbrain pages PENDING (next step)

corrections.what: No peat-btle#75 / aws-lc-rs basis
corrections.truth: No '#75' or 'aws-lc-rs' reference exists in peat-btle. aws-lc-rs in peat-flutter's lock is a TLS-stack transitive dep (rustls/rustls-webpki/tokio-websockets), unrelated to BLE crypto.
corrections.evidence: grep over peat-btle (zero hits); peat-flutter/rust/Cargo.lock:321-322 (aws-lc-rs via rustls)
corrections.status: applied (curriculum + ground-truth); gbrain pages PENDING (next step)

corrections.what: peat-mesh bypass.rs ChaCha20 is a stale doc comment
corrections.truth: peat-mesh shipping crypto is FIPS-clean (ADR-060 §5, PR #870). bypass.rs:365/:450 ChaCha20-Poly1305 mentions are STALE COMMENTS; the code encrypts with AES-256-GCM.

corrections.what: CellRole has 7 variants INCLUDING Support
corrections.truth: CellRole = {Leader, Sensor, Compute, Relay, Strike, Support, Follower} = 7 variants. The earlier ground-truth claiming 6 without Support is WRONG.

corrections.what: command_authority is a config string, not an enum; access ladder/retention are doc-only
corrections.truth: command_authority is a TOML operator-config string (C2_ONLY/ANY_TAK_USER, OPERATOR_GUIDE.md:931) — NOT a Rust enum. The Public(0)..Critical(4) access-level ladder and 90d/1y/2y audit-retention tiers are documentation-only (no code).
corrections.evidence: grep all *.rs: zero CommandAuthority enum / zero access-level ladder code

corrections.what: Partition 'original-leader-keeps-Cell-ID' is spec-only, NOT implemented
corrections.truth: Spec defines it (004-coordination.md:902-906) but peat-mesh only DETECTS partitions; cell-splitting / Cell-ID retention / minority-side new-ID are NOT implemented.

corrections.what: Automerge ActorId DeviceId[0:8]||SessionCounter is spec-only, NOT implemented
corrections.truth: Spec (002-sync.md:144-151) defines that construction; code uses Automerge's DEFAULT random ActorId (Automerge::new()/fork()), no DeviceId derivation.

corrections.what: peat-node is SINGLE-PORT (PEAT_NODE_LISTEN=50051); the 4040/8080/broker/recycle env vars do NOT exist
corrections.truth: peat-node binds one endpoint via PEAT_NODE_LISTEN (default tcp://0.0.0.0:50051) serving Connect+gRPC+gRPC-Web. There is NO PEAT_BIND_PORT(4040), PEAT_HTTP_PORT(8080), PEAT_BIND_ADDRESS, PEAT_BROKER_PORT, or PEAT_CONNECTION_RECYCLE_SECS. 8080 appears only as an example PEAT_NODE_AGENT_ADDR. It is brokerless P2P over Iroh QUIC.
corrections.evidence: peat-node/src/main.rs:38; docs/CONFIGURATION.md:11; grep zero for the named vars

corrections.what: peat-node RPC count = 27 defined / 27 implemented (full parity)
corrections.truth: proto/sidecar.proto defines exactly 27 RPCs; service.rs implements all 27. NOT 28/27 and NOT 25.

corrections.what: peat-node has NO X.509/PKI device-cert, NO CoT bind_port 8087, NO command_authority surface
corrections.truth: None of these exist in peat-node (not compiled, not documented). The only cert code is outbound TLS client trust. The PKI/CoT-8087/command_authority material belongs to a different component (peat-transport/operator guide) or is Speculative.

corrections.what: peat-schema hierarchy proto RENAMED — no SquadSummary/squad_id
corrections.truth: peat-schema/proto/hierarchy.proto now defines CellSummary/CohortSummary/FederationSummary/CoalitionSummary; zero Squad/squad_id. The 'peat-schema still ships SquadSummary' claim is STALE at HEAD 8a94796. (peat-btle src/lib.rs DOES still use legacy Platform/Squad — keep that distinct.)
corrections.status: applied (curriculum + ground-truth); gbrain pages PENDING (next step)

corrections.what: 11 ADRs Accepted (not only ADR-041); ADR-070 SAPIENT bridge is Accepted
corrections.truth: Accepted ADRs: 002, 009, 015, 016, 023, 024, 030, 041, 047, 057, 070 (=11). 'ADR-041 is the only Accepted ADR' is FALSE. ADR-070 (SAPIENT Protocol Bridge) is Accepted — peat-sapient is a first-class accepted integration.
corrections.status: applied (curriculum + ground-truth); gbrain pages PENDING (next step)

corrections.what: ADR-006 + ADR-044 are FIPS-amended (2026-05-18, PR #870)
corrections.truth: Both amended: ChaCha20-Poly1305 superseded by AES-256-GCM; ADR-044 MLS ciphersuite -> MLS_128_DHKEMP256_AES128GCM_SHA256_P256 (X25519 KE flagged for FIPS review). 'still carries pre-FIPS ChaCha20' is stale for 006/044. 048/049 + the peat-mesh/peat-btle READMEs are the still-stale docs.
corrections.status: applied (curriculum + ground-truth); gbrain pages PENDING (next step)

corrections.what: Tombstone-GC is SHIPPED in peat-node (not In-flight)
corrections.truth: peat-node ships tombstone GC: PEAT_NODE_TOMBSTONE_TTL_HOURS, gc_interval_secs, gc_batch_size (main.rs:109-122, wired :519-521, node.rs). peat-node#136 closed. Module 8 §8.8 'In-flight' is stale.

corrections.what: Per-collection deletion-policy config is a Shipped RPC surface (ADR-016)
corrections.truth: SetCollectionConfig/GetCollectionConfig/ListCollectionConfigs are Shipped RPCs (sidecar.proto:134-145) persisting DeletionPolicy+TTLs (ADR-016/peat-node#55) — distinct from #136. Module 8 conflates them.

corrections.what: ADR file count = 78 (not 75)
corrections.truth: ls peat/docs/adr/*.md = 78 (74 numbered ADRs + 4 reference docs).

corrections.what: PowerProfile has 4 variants incl Custom
corrections.truth: PowerProfile = Aggressive, Balanced(default), LowPower, Custom (config.rs:73). Module 4 says only 3. The 'no UltraLow code path' point still holds (UltraLow is not a variant).

corrections.what: QoS bandwidth shares: doc-comment vs shipped allocation differ
corrections.truth: Module 2.6 cites 40/30/20/8/2 (a doc-comment in qos/mod.rs). The shipped BandwidthAllocation uses guaranteed/burst shares (P1 20%/80% burst, P2 30%/60%, P3 20%/40%, ...) in qos/bandwidth.rs:250-300. Note the discrepancy.

corrections.what: Routing-enforcement file citation is wrong (file doesn't exist)
corrections.truth: Module 00b cites 'peat-mesh hierarchy/router.rs:19-20,90-91,140' (no such file) in 5 places. Real file: peat-mesh/src/routing/router.rs (route :246, Up/Down/Lateral :362-433, next-hop :487-533).

corrections.what: peat-node Remote Agent Watcher subsystem undocumented
corrections.truth: peat-node ships an opt-in agent watcher (off unless PEAT_NODE_AGENT_ADDR set) polling a local UDS Remote Agent over Connect RPC (watcher.rs, main.rs:127-145,584-599). Absent from Module 8.

corrections.what: peat-lite Document 0x07 wire layout leads with a flags byte
corrections.truth: Document 0x07 envelope = flags(1) | collection_len(1) | collection | doc_id... ; flags bit0=tombstone(0x01), bit1=encrypted(0x02), bits2-7 reserved (encoder rejects). Module 4 §4B.2 omits the leading flags field.

corrections.what: peat-btle beacon: two distinct advertising layouts
corrections.truth: beacon.rs documents manufacturer-data (40B) vs service-data (21B) layouts; the 16-byte body fits the 31-byte legacy limit as Service Data (3+18=21). Module 4 conflates them and reaches a wrong conclusion for the service-data case.

corrections.what: ground-truth/peat.md §0 stale crate versions
corrections.truth: §0 table says peat-protocol older + Cargo.lock peat-mesh rc.42; actual at HEAD: workspace rc.27, peat-ffi 0.2.8, Cargo.lock peat-mesh rc.43.

corrections.what: Repo inventory still says 'six repositories'
corrections.truth: Module 1 §1.4 + Module 7 §7.1 enumerate six repos; should be eight (add peat-flutter, peat-sapient). peat-sapient is ADR-070 Accepted.

corrections.what: Low-severity citation/labeling drift batch
corrections.truth: Misc: Module 2b FormationKey API source lines; Module 3 MeshCertificate wire format + transitive-gossip put_with_origin cite; Module 4 OTA lite_ota.rs location + per-profile battery framing + peat-lite header-byte labels. Fix citations/labels; low impact.
corrections.evidence: see discovery low-severity items in workflow result

verification_2026-06-26.ledgers_note: The dated audit-trail docs — review/ledger/*, review/claim-ledger.md, review/assumptions.md, review/gaps-contributions.md, review/CHANGELOG-review.md, review/diagrams.md — are point-in-time adversarial records. A few of their findings are now SUPERSEDED by this verification block (e.g. 'CellRole has no Support', 'sidecar.proto 28/27', 'peat-btle#75 aws-lc-rs'). They are intentionally LEFT as history (not rewritten); verification_2026-06-26 + the canonical ground-truth are authoritative.

self_improvement.mechanism: The loop self-CORRECTS curriculum drift every run (single-loop). To also self-IMPROVE (double-loop): the WEEKLY run consumes the reader-feedback signal (open curriculum-feedback GitHub issues), triages each against code, and records a run_log.meta scorecard + a quality_trend snapshot; the MONTHLY full sweep runs a retrospective (PROMPT-peat-training-doc-improvement.md §9b) that turns misses into PROPOSED prompt/gate amendments for HUMAN APPROVAL — a human-gated ratchet: tighten-only, evidence-required, never loosen a gate autonomously. EACH proposal is filed as a dedicated `prompt-amendment` GitHub issue (so it notifies + stays tracked, not buried in the refresh PR), with its number recorded in the amendment's `issue` field. Checks live in §1c (full) + gates 6-7 / Phase 0 step 2b / Phase 5 meta (incremental).

proposed_amendments.observed_miss: 'Code is truth' was read as git-HEAD; missed that the crates.io-published peat-btle 0.4.0 still ships ChaCha20/X25519 though source migrated (commit c8b013e)
proposed_amendments.proposed_check: Published-artifact != source: check consumer lockfiles + checksums; flag unpublished migrations; Shipped label states source-vs-published

proposed_amendments.observed_miss: Phantom peat-btle#75/aws-lc-rs propagated for weeks; 'only ADR-041 Accepted' and 'ADR count 75' carried forward unchecked
proposed_amendments.evidence: grep peat-btle for #75 = 0 hits; 11 ADRs Accepted; ls docs/adr/*.md = 78
proposed_amendments.proposed_check: Verify every repo#NNN in-repo; re-derive ADR status/counts each sweep; never carry forward an unconfirmed ref/count

proposed_amendments.observed_miss: Recurring facts (CellRole, SquadSummary, RPC count, router path) fixed in one file but missed in the hub mirror + 4-11 other spots
proposed_amendments.evidence: convergence found 27 residual occurrences after the first fix pass
proposed_amendments.proposed_check: Fact-wide occurrence sweep: grep ALL artifacts for every occurrence, fix/flag each

proposed_amendments.observed_miss: The process never improved itself; today's prompt hardening was a manual, human-directed double-loop pass with no standing mechanism
proposed_amendments.proposed_check: Standing self-improvement loop: weekly feedback-triage + run_log.meta scorecard + quality_trend; monthly §9b retrospective emitting human-gated proposed_amendments

quality_trend.note: baseline after the code-verification pass converged; curriculum-feedback label live but no reader issues yet. Both metrics should trend DOWN.

+1 unverifiable: the new peat-ffi reconnect/roster/origin surface + peat-mesh Android browse are code-confirmed but runtime-unverified (NEEDS_RUNTIME). The uptick is new-capability runtime claims, not unresolved drift — no prior verified claim regressed (misses_found=0). Still zero open feedback issues.

+2 unverifiable, both NEEDS_RUNTIME on THIS run's new capabilities: (1) the rc.45 direct blob path + rc.29 poll-based blob FFI + Android discovered-peer dial/browse-recovery; (2) the peat-sapient opt-in TLS negotiated key-exchange group (X25519 offered by construction, actual selection needs a runtime capture). Consistent with the 06-29 pattern — the climb is new-capability runtime claims, not unresolved drift or a regressed prior claim (misses_found=0). A fact-check CATCH this run (Accepted-ADR grep under-count 9 vs true 11, caused by a status-format variant) was corrected before shipping — no wrong count reached the docs. Still zero open feedback issues. NOTE for the monthly retrospective: the NEEDS_RUNTIME bucket keeps growing because the cloud refresh reads source but never runs a live build/benchmark; a standing runtime-verification harness (or an explicit 'runtime-unverified is expected, not a regression' carve-out in the trend metric) would stop this from reading as the loop failing to learn.
